message:
So there I was last month during a storm—power flickering, kids asking a thousand questions—and I realized my phone was at 3%. 3%! Of course that's when I needed to check the weather alert, text my mom we were okay, and honestly just have *something* to do while we waited it out.

Turned out I had a power bank buried in a drawer (from like 2019), and it actually saved the day. Got my phone through the outage, kept the kids entertained for a bit, and I could actually stay informed instead of just sitting there hoping.

Now I keep a charged power bank in our emergency kit. Sounds simple, but that moment taught me it's not just about having *stuff*—it's about keeping the things that actually matter to us running when the grid doesn't.

Do you keep yours charged, or are you like I was? 😄
